Notice And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Motion To Stay Litigation (C.C.P. 1281.2, 1281.4)
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT RODGERS STREET, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration; Motion To Stay Litigation (C.C.P. 1281.2, 1281.4).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Rodgers Street, LLC's motion to compel arbitration and stay litigation is denied for lack of an arbitration agreement. "Arbitration is a matter of contract and a party cannot be required to submit to arbitration any dispute which he has not agreed so to submit." (United Steelworkers of America v. Warrior & Gulf Nav. Co. (1960) 363 U.S. 574, 582; Grey v. American Management Services (2012) 204 Cal.App.4th 803, 805-809.) The party seeking arbitration bears the burden of proving the existence of an arbitration agreement. (Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. v. Pinnacle Market Development (US), LLC (2012) 55 Cal.4th 223, 236.) Absent such an agreement, a court may not grant a motion to compel arbitration.

Defendant argues that the parties' 2018 construction contract contains an arbitration provision, specifically Article 13 entitled, "Dispute Resolution." (See Cyrus Sanandaji Decl., Ex. A.) The court disagrees. Article 13 of the contract does not contain an agreement to arbitrate. Instead, it provides a procedure for resolving certain claims and disputes on an "interim" basis prior to the final completion of the project. (Cyrus Sanandaji Decl., Ex. A ["Such decision shall be binding upon the parties until Final Completion of the Project, but shall thereafter be subject to final adjudication pursuant to a full proceeding under Section Article 18 herein."].) Neither does Article 18 does contain an arbitration provision. Article 18 ("Waiver to Right to Jury Trial") discusses and acknowledges that pre-dispute waivers of jury trials are invalid citing to Grafton Partners LP v. Superior Court (2005) 36 Cal.4th 944 and that the parties agree to resolve claims by way of judicial reference.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
